title: v66 — solid walls + enter-through-doors, readable chat

items.title: World map shows the REAL island
items.how: Click World Map (top right): you see the whole Tutorial Island in the ocean - all nine zone names (Chapel, Warden's Lodge, Mine, Combat Yard, Bank, Wizard's Tower, Departure Dock...), building footprints, the winding dirt path, bank/furnace/anvil icons, red creature dots, and a 'You' marker at your actual position. Pan + zoom work.

items.title: Equip/unequip end-to-end
items.how: Tap a sword in the bag: it equips AND appears in your hand. Open Worn Equipment and tap the weapon cell: it unequips, returns to the bag, and disappears from your hand.

items.title: You start at level 1 (dev character OFF)
items.how: Log in fresh: skills are level 1 (not 99), no wall of level-up popups, a small starter bag. The maxed dev character is now opt-in via ?dev in the URL.

items.title: Bank booth opens the bank
items.how: Walk to a bank booth and click it: the Bank of Eldermoor panel opens (tabs, search, deposit/withdraw). Works whether you click from far away or standing right next to it.

items.title: Clicking adjacent things works instantly
items.how: Stand right next to an NPC / furnace / anvil / booth and click it: the action happens immediately (talk/smelt/bank) - no more silent dead click.

items.title: Solid fixtures are clickable
items.how: Furnace, anvil, bank booth: clicking them routes you beside them and performs the action - no more permanent 'I can't reach that.'

items.title: Click a rat -> real fight
items.how: Find the Giant Rat near the chapel (or rat pen). Click it: your character walks over, swings (attack animation), hitsplats + an HP bar appear, the rat flashes red when hit and keels over when it dies.

items.title: Locked combat explains itself
items.how: If your character hasn't done the melee lesson, clicking a rat shows a clear banner mid-screen telling you which instructor to visit - NOT silent nothing.

items.title: Mobs are alive
items.how: Watch a rat/chicken for ~10s: it wanders around its spawn, legs scurrying, tail swaying, and it turns to face you during a fight.

items.title: Chickens look like chickens
items.how: The practice chicken is a white bird with a red comb and beak - not a brown rat.

items.title: Near-miss taps still attack
items.how: Tap slightly OFF a small rat: it still attacks (OSRS forgiving tap) instead of walking to the spot.

items.title: Creator shows the REAL character
items.how: Fresh character: the creator preview is the actual 3D game character (idle-breathing, rotating), NOT a blocky box figure.

items.title: Skin tone + hair colour apply
items.how: In the creator, click different Skin and Hair swatches: the model's face/hands and hair visibly change colour immediately.

items.title: Head/torso picks change the model
items.how: Cycle Head to Hooded (or Torso to Robe): the preview swaps to the hooded/robed character and keeps your chosen colours.

items.title: In-world character matches the creator
items.how: Enter the world: your character has the same skin tone/hair colour you picked, and is NOT holding an armory of extra swords/shields.

items.title: Equipping is visible
items.how: Wield a sword/shield from the bag: it appears in the character's hand; unequip removes it. Try a helm/cape if you have one.

items.title: NPCs carry role gear only
items.how: Look at instructors: the guard-types hold sword+shield, the mage a staff — nobody is holding several shields at once.

items.title: Login screen on load
items.how: On load a login/landing screen appears; type a name and Enter Eldermoor starts the game.

items.title: Logout returns to login
items.how: Open the Logout tab → Logout: it returns to the login screen, not a dead/blank screen.

items.title: XP counter no longer overlaps inventory
items.how: Gain XP with the inventory open — the XP-gained numbers don't sit on top of the inventory.

items.title: Chat box always visible
items.how: A chat box is visible at bottom-left and stays usable even with the inventory/tab panel open.

items.title: Stat orbs placed cleanly
items.how: HP/Prayer/Run/Spec orbs sit in a tidy cluster (under the minimap), not overlapping anything.

items.title: Proper tab dock
items.how: The tabs are a fixed docked strip (no random floating 'Tabs' button up top).

items.title: Warm brown-stone tone
items.how: The UI reads warm brown-stone (OSRS-like), not blue everywhere.

items.title: Minimap rebuilt
items.how: Minimap has a compass, click-to-walk, zoom (scroll/pinch), and coloured blips.

items.title: Combat styles explain + train
items.how: Combat tab lists styles with what each trains; XP goes to the matching skill; styles change when you equip a different weapon (bow vs sword).

items.title: Full prayer roster
items.how: Prayer tab shows a large roster (~28) with level requirements; locked ones greyed.

items.title: Spellbook tiers + runes
items.how: Magic tab shows strike/bolt/blast/wave tiers + teleports/alchemy, each with rune requirements; greyed when you lack runes/level.

items.title: Dialogue dismiss + new chat
items.how: Walking away from an NPC closes the dialogue; you can immediately talk to a different NPC; Esc/X also closes it.

items.title: Dev toolbox
items.how: Press backtick (`) or the 🛠 button: a dev panel opens to give items, set skills/HP/prayer, give runes, teleport, and spawn a mob.

items.title: Dev kit complete
items.how: The dev character has all runes + a spread of gear tiers + tools to test everything.

items.title: Gear tiers exist
items.how: Multiple gear tiers exist beyond bronze (Ferrite→Steel→Blacksteel→Duskstone→Verdigrant→Sunsteel) — give yourself some via the dev toolbox.

items.title: QA live sync
items.how: As you tap Pass/Fail/notes, results sync to the dev dashboard.

items.title: Combat: walk-to-mob + death/respawn
items.how: Attack a distant mob: the character walks to it then fights; if you die you respawn at spawn; the mob dies and respawns.

items.title: Combat animation
items.how: Attacking shows a swing/lunge; dying shows a fall.

items.title: Skill guide opens
items.how: Open the Stats tab and click a skill — a guide popup opens (what unlocks at which level) + xp-to-next.

items.title: Tutorial completes on the action
items.how: A lesson completes when you DO the task (e.g. chop a tree), not the instant the instructor stops talking.

items.title: Per-zone music + settings persist
items.how: Music changes between zones with a crossfade; Settings audio mute/volume persist across reload.

items.title: Higher spells castable
items.how: With runes from the dev kit/toolbox, you can cast the higher spells (blast/wave/teleport).

items.title: Unique per-zone music
items.how: Each zone has its OWN melodic theme (lead+bass+arp+perc), not a drone; themes crossfade on zone change; jukebox (Music tab) plays each.

items.title: Walk the whole island
items.how: Walk north from the chapel through all 9 zones (survival, cooks, quest, mine, combat, bank, wizard, dock); each has a building/fixtures and a named instructor you can talk to (correct dialogue).

items.title: 3D creator preview
items.how: In the character creator the preview is a rotating 3D model of your real avatar (not a flat paper-doll); it updates live as you change head/torso/arms/legs/feet/colours/body type.

items.title: Real 3D character (not blocky)
items.how: After creating your character, the in-world body is a real rigged low-poly model (KayKit) that walks/attacks/dies with real animations; equipped weapon/shield appear in the hands. Not the Minecraft box.

items.title: Use item on item/object
items.how: Use tinderbox on logs -> fire; flour on water -> dough; raw shrimp on fire -> cooked; ore on furnace -> bar; bar on anvil -> dagger.

items.title: Instructors are real 3D models
items.how: The instructor NPCs are rigged KayKit characters (idle/walk animated) like your player, not boxes.
